comment: Dolt-shaped fixture rows for the pipeline e2e test (tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py),
served by bin/dolt to the real `mem build-store --with-traces` CLI. Shape mirrors
`dolt sql -r json` output: every value a string, NULL columns omitted (ingest/beads.ts
DoltRow). Five works across two rigs, all of whose transcripts plant the SAME failure
signature, so D6 exclusion is the only thing separating retrievable from excluded:
B           rigA  the query work (started 2026-06-10, external_ref branch/feat-x)
prior-cross rigB  closed before B.started -> the cross-rig match
prior-same  rigA  closed before B.started -> the same-rig match
future      rigB  closed AFTER B.started  -> temporal LOO exclusion
sibling     rigB  shares B's external_ref -> branch-sibling exclusion

queries.comment: The exact SQL ingest/beads.ts sends (RIGS_SQL/ISSUES_SQL/LABELS_SQL), byte-pinned like PLANTED_SIGNATURE: dolt's NULL-omission makes a missing column shape-valid, so a looser match would let an ingest SQL change silently decay e2e coverage. An ingest-side query change costs a one-line bump here — deliberately.
queries.rigs: select table_schema as rig from information_schema.tables where table_name = 'issues'
queries.issues: select id, title, status, assignee, external_ref, priority, created_at, started_at, closed_at, metadata from issues
